png

Class PngParser

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>
      • png.PngParser

  • public class PngParser
extends AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>
    Parses PNG image files to extract metadata structured within sequential chunks. Processes textual chunks (tEXt, iTXt, zTXt), modification time (tIME), and embedded EXIF (eXIf) data blocks using a structured internal data holder.

    PNG Data Stream Structure

    The PNG data stream begins with an 8-byte PNG SIGNATURE (0x89 0x50 0x4E 0x47 0x0D 0x0A 0x1A 0x0A) followed by a series of chunks. Each individual chunk consists of:

    • 4 bytes: Data field length (unsigned 31-bit integer)
    • 4 bytes: Chunk type identifier (restricted to [65-90] and [97-122] ASCII codes)
    • Variable bytes: The actual chunk data payload
    • 4 bytes: CRC-32 checksum computed from the chunk type and data fields only

    There are two categories of chunks: Critical (required for rendering) and Ancillary (metadata).

    Critical Chunk Types

    • IHDR - Image header; must be the first chunk in the data stream.
    • PLTE - Palette table; required for indexed-colour PNG images.
    • IDAT - Image data chunk; multiple sequential instances are common.
    • IEND - Image trailer; marks the end of the PNG stream.

    Ancillary Chunk Types

    • Transparency info: tRNS
    • Colour space info: cHRM, gAMA, iCCP, sBIT, sRGB, cICP, mDCV
    • Textual info: iTXt, tEXt, zTXt
    • Miscellaneous info: bKGD, hIST, pHYs, sPLT, eXIf
    • Time info: tIME
    • Animation information: acTL, fcTL, fdAT

    Note: In Windows Explorer, the Date Taken attribute is often resolved preferentially from the Creation Time textual keyword rather than an embedded EXIF block. This behaviour can alter the chronological ordering of PNG files when processed or sorted on Windows filesystems. 

     -- For developmental testing --

 Examples of helpful exiftool diagnostic recipes:

 # View all time blocks categorized by group
 exiftool -time:all -a -G0:1 -s testPNGimage.png

 # Explicitly overwrite all standard timestamps
 exiftool.exe -overwrite_original -alldates="2012:10:07 11:15:45" testPNGimage.png

 # Synchronise the filesystem modification date with the internal PNG metadata value
 exiftool.exe "-FileModifyDate
    Since:
    13 August 2025
    Version:
    1.1
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs
    See Also:
    W3C Portable Network Graphics (PNG) Specification

      • PngParser

        public PngParser(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a new instance with the specified file path.
        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the PNG file as an encapsulated object
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened or read

      • PngParser

        public PngParser(java.lang.String file)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a new instance from a file path string.
        Parameters:
        file - the path to the PNG file as a string
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file cannot be opened or read

    • Method Detail

      • readMetadata

        public void readMetadata()
        Parses the PNG file to extract filtered metadata chunks, such as EXIF, textual properties, and embedded XMP data and populates the internal directories.
        Specified by:
        readMetadata in class AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>

      • getMetadata

        public PngMetadata getMetadata()
        Retrieves the extracted metadata from the PNG image file. If the metadata has not been explicitly loaded yet, it triggers lazy execution to read data.
        Specified by:
        getMetadata in class AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>
        Returns:
        a PngMetadata object populated with extracted directories, or empty

      • getImageFormat

        public DigitalSignature getImageFormat()
        Returns the detected PNG format.
        Specified by:
        getImageFormat in class AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>
        Returns:
        DigitalSignature.PNG

      • formatDiagnosticString

        public java.lang.String formatDiagnosticString()
        Generates a human-readable diagnostic string containing metadata details.
        Overrides:
        formatDiagnosticString in class AbstractImageParser<PngMetadata>
        Returns:
        a formatted string suitable for diagnostics, logging, or inspection

      • loadMetadata

        private PngParser.PngChunkData loadMetadata(java.util.EnumSet<ChunkType> chunkFilter)
                                     throws java.io.IOException
        Loads the filtered metadata chunks from the PNG image file.
        Parameters:
        chunkFilter - a filter set of chunk types to allow specific chunks to be processed
        Returns:
        a PngChunkData record containing the extracted chunks
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an unrecoverable issue occurs while accessing the filesystem or reading the stream
